Participant.title: Participant
Participant.description: Represents a single participant or the team leader who registered for the hackathon, storing their personal, academic, team, and payment information. This entity is the main record for each registration.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Participant entity.

fullName.description: The full name of the participant or team leader.

email.description: The primary email address of the participant.

phone.description: The phone number of the participant, including country code.

gender.description: The gender of the participant (e.g., male, female, other).

dateOfBirth.description: The participant's date of birth in YYYY-MM-DD format (within 2000-2010 range).

college.description: The name of the participant's college or university.

degree.description: The degree program the participant is pursuing (e.g., B.Tech, B.E.).

branch.description: The academic branch or department of the participant (e.g., Computer Science, Electrical Engineering).

year.description: The current year of study for the participant (e.g., 1st Year, 2nd Year, 3rd Year, Final Year).

teamName.description: The name of the participant's team.

teamLeaderName.description: The name of the team leader. This will typically match the fullName of this Participant entity.

numberOfTeamMembers.description: The total number of members in the team, including the team leader. (Value is 1 + count of referenced TeamMember entities).

teamMemberIds.description: References to other TeamMember entities associated with this participant's team, excluding the team leader. (Relationship: Participant 1:N TeamMember)

linkedinProfile.description: URL to the participant's LinkedIn profile.

githubProfile.description: URL to the participant's GitHub profile.

portfolioWebsite.description: URL to the participant's personal portfolio website.

paymentStatus.description: The current payment status of the registration (e.g., pending, completed, failed).

currency.description: The currency code for the registration fee (e.g., INR).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the participant's registration record was first created.

submittedAt.description: Timestamp when the participant's registration data was fully submitted.

TeamMember.title: TeamMember
TeamMember.description: Represents an individual team member, typically excluding the main participant/team leader who registered the team. This entity stores details for additional team members.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TeamMember entity.

name.description: The full name of the team member.

email.description: The email address of the team member.

participantId.description: Reference to the Participant entity who registered this team member. This establishes a 1:N relationship where one Participant can register multiple TeamMembers. (Relationship: Participant 1:N TeamMember)

definition.description: Represents the main participant or team leader who registered. The document ID `{participantId}` MUST match the `request.auth.uid` of the registering user, and the `id` field within the document also matches this `participantId`. Includes references to `TeamMember` IDs in the `teamMemberIds` array.

params.name: participantId
params.description: The unique ID of the participant, which must correspond to the Firebase Authentication UID of the registering user.

definition.description: Represents an individual team member registered by a participant. The `{participantId}` in the path refers to the registering team leader, and the `participantId` field within this document is denormalized to match the parent `participantId` for authorization independence. The `{teamMemberId}` is the unique ID for this specific team member document.

params.name: participantId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent participant (team leader) who registered this team member. This must match the `request.auth.uid` for authorized access.

params.name: teamMemberId
params.description: The unique ID of this specific team member document.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC) to identify administrators. The existence of a document at `/admins/{request.auth.uid}` grants elevated privileges (e.g., viewing all participant data) without relying on custom claims. Documents typically contain minimal or no data, relying on existence for authorization checks.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique ID of an authenticated user designated as an administrator. This must correspond to the Firebase Authentication UID.

firestore.reasoning: The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Query-Aware Security Rules (QAPs) by structuring data explicitly around ownership and segregating access patterns.The core `Participant` entity will reside in the `/participants/{participantId}` collection, where `participantId` is designed to be the authenticated user's unique ID (`request.auth.uid`). This ensures that each participant implicitly owns and can only access their own registration record, fulfilling the "Private Data" access model. The `id` field within the `Participant` document will also match this `participantId`, reinforcing self-ownership and simplifying security rules significantly by eliminating the need for `get()` calls to determine ownership.Team members, represented by the `TeamMember` entity, are stored in a subcollection: `/participants/{participantId}/teamMembers/{teamMemberId}`. This hierarchical path directly links team members to their registering participant/team leader. To ensure Authorization Independence for `TeamMember` documents, each `TeamMember` document will denormalize its `participantId` from its parent `Participant`. This allows rules applied to the `teamMembers` subcollection to verify ownership simply by comparing `request.auth.uid` with the parent `participantId` (from the path) or the `participantId` field within the `TeamMember` document itself, again avoiding `get()` calls.QAPs are supported through structural segregation. Normal users (participants) will only be able to query (list and get) their own `Participant` document and its associated `TeamMember` subcollection, as the `participantId` in the path directly corresponds to their `request.auth.uid`. For the administrative "Real-time Participant Dashboard" requiring access to *all* participant data, a separate collection `/admins/{adminId}` is introduced. Documents in this collection simply signify an administrator's `uid`. Security rules can then check for the existence of `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/admins/$(request.auth.uid))` to grant broader read/write access to the entire `/participants` collection (and its subcollections) for authenticated administrators. This approach clearly separates user-specific data access from global administrative access, preventing rules from acting as filters and maintaining performance and predictability.The `Participant` document's `teamMemberIds` array will store the `id` values of the `TeamMember` documents in the subcollection, providing a clear reference while avoiding embedding complex objects, thereby maintaining predictable schemas and adhering to the `TeamMember` entity's definition.
